title: Rsync Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Rsync commands for efficient file synchronization and transfer.

commands.description: Synchronize files from local to remote with compression.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to back up a local directory to a remote server efficiently.

commands.description: Synchronize directories and delete extraneous files from destination.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to mirror a directory and remove files not present in the source.

commands.description: Use a specific SSH port for remote synchronization.
commands.scenario: Use when the remote server SSH is running on a non-standard port.
Use when you want to see detailed progress of the file transfer operation.

commands.description: Exclude specific file patterns from synchronization.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to skip temporary files during synchronization.

commands.description: Perform a trial run with no changes made.
commands.scenario: Use to verify what changes will be made without actually performing the synchronization.

commands.description: Limit the bandwidth used during transfer.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to control the network bandwidth used by rsync.

commands.description: Keep partially transferred files to resume later.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to resume interrupted transfers without starting over.

commands.description: Use hard links to save space with incremental backups.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to create efficient incremental backups.

commands.description: Set permissions on the destination files and directories.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to ensure specific permissions on synchronized files.

commands.description: Backup files that are replaced during synchronization.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to keep backups of files that are overwritten during sync.

commands.description: Copy symlinks as the referent files.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to copy the files symlinks point to, not the symlinks themselves.
